description_long: This object is a standard North American duplex electrical wall outlet, featuring two vertically aligned three-prong receptacles set into a rectangular, slightly beveled faceplate. The faceplate is smooth, glossy, and white, with a single screw hole at the top center for mounting. Each outlet has two parallel vertical slots and a round grounding hole below, all outlined by subtle beveled edges. The side views reveal the outlet's shallow depth and the gentle curvature of the faceplate, which tapers toward the wall. The back and side surfaces are plain, with no visible openings or features. This type of outlet is commonly found in residential, commercial, and urban environments for providing AC electrical power.
description: A white, glossy duplex electrical wall outlet with two three-prong receptacles and a beveled rectangular faceplate.
description_view_0: Front view showing two three-prong electrical receptacles and a mounting screw on a glossy white faceplate.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the outlet's shallow depth and the gentle curvature of the faceplate.
description_view_2: Rear or top-down view highlighting the smooth, featureless back of the faceplate.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and curved edges of the outlet.
